description_long: This object is a medium-sized airplane with a straight, rectangular fuselage and a set of wings extending from either side. The exterior appears to be primarily metallic, with visible markings and some weathering, suggesting it is used and operational. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a classic tail design. The landing gear is visible and appears to be of the tricycle type, with two main wheels and a nose wheel. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage and is enclosed, with windows visible in some views. The overall color scheme is a mix of muted grays and browns, with some lighter and darker patches indicating wear or painted markings.
description: A medium-sized, metallic airplane with a straight fuselage, conventional tail, tricycle landing gear, and a forward enclosed cockpit. The exterior is mostly gray and brown with visible markings and signs of use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose, cockpit windows, and the word 'SUSU' on the fuselage; landing gear and part of the wing are visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the wing extending outward, and the cockpit area; landing gear and tail are visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the tail section, vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the back of the wings; some landing gear detail is visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the wing, fuselage, and tail visible; cockpit and landing gear are also apparent.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engines; engine type and placement not clearly shown.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed and non-retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with visible windows, likely single or dual seat.
